preamble: The shift from [r] to [ə] is a form of vocalization where the rhotic consonant loses its trill or tap quality and centralizes into a neutral vowel. This is a defining feature of non-rhotic accents in English and Standard German.

examples.note: Final r vocalizes to schwa in non-rhotic dialects.
German coda r is almost always vocalized to a near-open central vowel (Tiefschwa).
